scope_note: Findings classify the architecture registry (prd/architecture/) only. Source-of-truth remains with PRD/GSD/ADR/source/runtime evidence. Findings are derived observations, not proof.

field_alignment.note: Findings use a subset of architecture.schema.json fields so they can be promoted into architecture_items.jsonl as proof_gate / risk / evidence records later.

sections.title: Registry summary and structural metrics
sections.description: Overview of artifacts in prd/architecture/, node/edge counts, layer coverage, and high-level posture.

sections.title: Strengths to preserve
sections.description: Mature patterns in the registry that should not regress (non_claims discipline, proof_level ladder, ADR metadata, fail-closed verifier, schema safeguards).

sections.title: Inconsistencies and registry-quality issues
sections.description: Concrete defects in items/edges/views: missing layers, orphan nodes, low coverage, weakly connected components, duplicated non_claims, schema-version drift risk.

sections.title: Architectural risks of the registry itself
sections.description: Higher-order risks: governance-vs-product imbalance, .gsd path coupling, verifier scope, schema version evolution, traceability gap to prd/parser/.

sections.title: Missing record classes and coverage gaps
sections.description: Record kinds and content that should exist in the registry but are absent (interface, data_entity, component, quality_scenario, viewpoint, FR-coverage, edge type usage).

sections.title: Action plan by priority
sections.description: BLOCKER / MAJOR / MINOR action items with explicit linkage to the inconsistency, risk, or gap they resolve.

non_claims: Does not validate registry runtime behavior.
Does not assert product readiness for LegalGraph Nexus.
Does not promote any finding into an architecture_items.jsonl record automatically.
Does not assert that resolving these findings is sufficient for product proof.
